title: aquilegia micrantha subsp. ecalcarata
intro: Aquilegia micrantha subsp. ecalcarata, commonly known as the alpine columbine or Rocky Mountain columbine, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Aquilegia micrantha subsp. ecalcarata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural remedy for colds and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making a tea from its flowers and leaves, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
